title: Redesign Clear Filter Button UX with Contextual Label Toggle
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the 'Clear' button to be contextual — showing 'Filters' when no filters are active and 'Clear Filters' as a link when filters are applied — so I can clearly distinguish the action from the Close button.
description:
UX improvement to the Reports/Dashboards filter panel:

1. By default 'Clear' button is disabled and the label reads 'Filters'
2. When user selects a View or sets any filter (including search), the 'Filters' label is hidden and replaced with 'Clear Filters' as a clickable link
3. When filters are cleared, it reverts back to 'Filters' label
4. This saves space and prevents confusion between Clear and Close buttons
5. Minor cleanup: capitalize 'views' to 'Views'
acceptance_criteria: Default state shows 'Filters' label with no clear action
When any filter is active, label changes to 'Clear Filters' clickable link
Close button remains separate and unambiguous
ux_changes: Replaced the separate Clear button with a contextual label that toggles between 'Filters' (static label) and 'Clear Filters' (active link) based on filter state. Capitalized 'Views'.
implementation_notes: JavaScript listens for filter changes (dropdown, search, view selection) and toggles the label/link. Deployed to dev, stage, and prod.
comment_summary: Waqar implemented the toggle behavior and deployed to all 3 environments. Screenshots show before (static label) and after (clear link) states.
